definition: I2S materializes a multi-byte Thread/IPv6 protocol-header template or network-address constant (6–32 bytes: mesh-local prefix, routing-locator IID anchor, multicast address, or IPv6 next-header template) from the CMP buffer, satisfying address-equality predicates or producing a specific error code in the Thread packet-processing stack.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

members.function: ot::Message::GetFirstChunk(unsigned short, unsigned short&, ot::Message::Chunk&) const

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace materializes the multi-byte IPv6 protocol-header template (32 bytes of concentrated literals) from CMP buffer operands in one mutation, pushing aOffset past kBufferDataSize to reach the false branch of the range check.
signature.one_line: I2S materializes fixed IPv6 header template to exceed buffer size threshold

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a stable multi-byte literal at offsets 0x00-0x1f that upstream IP6/checksum parser equality CMPs accept, so a long enough multi-buffer message reaches GetFirstChunk with aOffset >= kBufferDataSize after the head-buffer subtraction.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes at offsets 0x00-0x1f must match a fixed multi-byte literal sequence (the 5 winner seeds share `78 66 65 5c 78` at 0x00-0x04 and dozens of single-valued offsets through 0x1f) so that upstream IP6 datagram parsing / checksum validation accepts the packet and feeds it into GetFirstChunk with an aOffset that, after subtracting kHeadBufferDataSize at line 513, still exceeds kBufferDataSize — forcing the loop to subtract `aOffset -= kBufferDataSize` (lines 529-530, hit only by W) before the branch ever evaluates true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the exact same first-32-bytes template (BYTE DIFF shows offsets 0x00-0x07, 0x0d-0x11, 0x16-0x1f each concentrated to a single byte value xN10), demonstrating they share a fixed protocol-header literal. The per-role overlay marks lines 529-530 (`aOffset -= kBufferDataSize`) as [W]-only — direct evidence the winner traverses the multi-buffer subtract path and reaches the false branch, while the loser never does.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (all from naive) have heterogeneous bytes at the same offsets (e.g. offset 0x02 shows 24($)x3 23(#)x3 10(.)x2 2e(.)x2 in L vs 65(e)x10 in W; offset 0x1a shows 53(S)x10 in L vs de(.)x10 in W) — no concentrated multi-byte literal. The DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows L hits the blocker line with T=176 F=0: every L invocation takes the TRUE branch (aOffset fits in one buffer) because the message never has enough bytes to push aOffset past kBufferDataSize after the head subtraction. Value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient is Hamming-bucketed and cannot collapse a multi-byte equality match in one step; it must accumulate byte-by-byte.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The pair delta is I2SRandReplace: value_profile_cmplog adds the cmplog CMP buffer and the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of value_profile's existing instrumentation. The upstream IP6/checksum parser performs multi-byte equality CMPs against fixed header literals; I2SRandReplace reads those operands out of the CMP buffer and substitutes them into the input verbatim, materializing the multi-byte template in a single mutation. Without I2SRandReplace, value_profile must synthesize the multi-byte literal via blind havoc + token mutators, and the absolute search cost for a 32-byte concentrated pattern is what produces the 0/10 resolve rate.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an inequality compare inside the multi-buffer chunk-walk loop of ot::Message::GetFirstChunk; the blocked side is the false branch (aOffset >= kBufferDataSize after the head-subtract).
The blocked side at this branch is the false (F) branch.
The only decisive pair at this branch is value_profile_cmplog > value_profile with I2S as the technique delta.
vpc resolves every trial; vp blocks every trial — prob_div is 1.00 and the loser never escapes the gate.
The per-role overlay marks line 529 (`aOffset -= kBufferDataSize`) as hit only by winner-resolving seeds — direct evidence W traverses the multi-buffer subtract path and L does not.
The loop-iteration step at line 530 is also W-only, confirming W is the only side that re-enters the while loop after the first chunk and falls into the false branch.
At the blocker line, loser invocations always take the true branch (T=176 F=0) while winner takes both (T=47 F=35) — the false side is exactly the side L never reaches.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 523  T=47 F=35  T=176 F=0  if (aOffset < kBufferDataSize)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: All five winner-resolving seeds share the same first five bytes `78 66 65 5c 78` (ASCII `xfe\x`), each concentrated to a single value across all 10 reads.
The stable winner literal extends through offsets 0x01-0x04 — each concentrated to a single byte value across all 10 W reads while L is heterogeneous.
The winner template also pins offset 0x1a to `de` x10 while L pins it to `53` (ASCII 'S') x10 — different stable literals on each side, but the W side is the one upstream parser accepts.
The first winner seed shows the characteristic pattern visually: `78 66 65 5c 78 00 10 2c b0 78 da 30 5c 78 30 30` at offsets 0x00-0x0f, all repeated across the five W seeds.
value_profile_cmplog has I2SRandReplace as part of its mutator set on top of CMP_MAP gradient and the cmplog CMP buffer.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; it has only edge feedback + CMP_MAP gradient + havoc/token mutators.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates a multi-buffer Message chunk-walk behind a single equality CMP on the same multi-byte literal would, under I2S attribution, show vpc resolving the false branch while vp blocks. If at literal-width MAGIC_BYTES=4 (or higher) value_profile resolves the loop's false branch as quickly as value_profile_cmplog, the I2SRandReplace attribution is wrong and CMP_MAP gradient alone suffices.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The I2S-floor verification was performed on a cmplog (REFERENCE) seed because seed_bisect stored no value_profile_cmplog winning seeds for this branch; the I2SRandReplace attribution for the decisive vpc>vp pair is therefore inferred from the shared I2S delta plus the matching mechanism context, not directly observed on a vpc lineage.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace reads the 16-byte Thread multicast address constant from the CMP buffer and pastes it into the IPv6 destination field of the input, satisfying the address equality that naive cannot synthesize from blind havoc.
signature.one_line: I2S pastes 16-byte Thread multicast address into IPv6 destination field

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode an IPv6 multicast destination equal to ff32::1 or ff33::1 (Thread well-known all-thread-nodes addresses).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The IPv6 header destination field (16 bytes) must compare-equal to one of two well-known Thread multicast addresses: LinkLocalAllThreadNodes (ff32:...:0001) or RealmLocalAllThreadNodes (ff33:...:0001). The seeds must first encode a Type=Ip6 message with a multicast destination not larger than realm-local and not MPL-retransmission subtype, then place the 16-byte multicast address literal at the destination offset of the embedded IPv6 header.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the 16-byte FF32-prefixed multicast address literal verbatim — e.g. seed dfad99a60a0fc48e contains 'ff 32 00 40 fd de ad 00 be ef 00 00 00 00 00 01' at offsets 0x18-0x27 and seed ca90f41f741f4e23 contains the same pattern at 0x19-0x28, exactly matching the operand returned by mle.GetLinkLocalAllThreadNodesAddress().
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds reach the same site (line 81 fires T=0 F=67 in L) but the destination bytes do not match either fixed multicast address; their payloads after the header offset are dominated by zero runs or unrelated bytes (e.g. seed 009333ba20c255f4 is mostly NULs at the address position), so both 16-byte equality comparisons fail and control flows to the unicast-else / for-loop path at lines 96-103.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's CMP instrumentation intercepts the 16-byte address equality (a memcmp / Address::operator== chain). I2SRandReplace then reads the per-execution CMP buffer, locates the runtime operand (the well-known multicast address constant retrieved from mle), and substitutes it into matching offsets of the input, planting the literal address bytes naive cannot synthesize from blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a 128-bit IPv6 destination address equality comparison against a Thread well-known multicast constant.
A second equality with the realm-local-all-thread-nodes address gates the same arm; both constants must be matched (one of two).
The branch is preceded by a multicast-and-not-larger-than-realm-local gate, so the destination must already be a small-scope multicast address before the equality check fires.
The winning seed dfad99a60a0fc48e contains the FF32-prefixed multicast address literal verbatim within its bytes.
The same FF32-prefixed multicast pattern appears in the second cmplog-trial-1 winner, ending in 00 01 — exactly the canonical LinkLocalAllThreadNodes terminator.
Loser seeds reach the comparison but never take the true branch — line 81 fires T=2 F=12 in W versus T=0 F=67 in L.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L  81  T=2 F=12  T=0 F=67  if (ip6Header.GetDestination() == mle.GetLinkLocalAllThre...

evidence_trail.claim: The cmplog vs naive pair is admissible and decisive at this branch with prob_div=0.80 and subject p_AUC=0.0002.
I2SRandReplace works by substituting one CMP operand into the input at offsets matching the other operand, which is how a 16-byte fixed multicast address constant from a runtime call gets planted.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: Naive has no CMP introspection and only havoc/token mutations, so it cannot synthesize a 16-byte fixed multicast literal except by improbable random construction.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a 16-byte equality CMP against a fixed constant where cmplog fails to resolve significantly more often than naive within the same budget — i.e. observing naive-vs-cmplog resolution probability difference <0.2 at MAGIC_BYTES=16 with default trial duration would falsify the I2S-substitution attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The 16-byte multicast address may be wired through a method call chain (Address::operator==) rather than a single memcmp callback; if cmplog's CMP instrumentation does not actually intercept this chain as a single 16-byte operand pair, the I2S signal would have to come from a smaller sub-comparison and the dose-response curve under MAGIC_BYTES=16 might behave differently than predicted.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 8-byte IPv6 header constant (including next-header 0x11 = kProtoUdp) from the CMP buffer, routing the message into the UDP/ICMP6 handler that returns a non-kErrorNone error, enabling the true branch at line 981.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 8-byte IPv6 header literal to produce non-kErrorNone error

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry a fixed IPv6-header byte prefix that routes the packet through UDP/ICMP6 handlers whose handlers return a non-kErrorNone status.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A fixed multi-byte IPv6-header prefix at offsets 0x00-0x07 (W seeds share `78 66 65 5c 7[7-8] 00 10 11`, where 0x11 = kProtoUdp at the next-header byte) that lets HandlePayload reach the UDP/ICMP6 dispatch and have the chosen handler return a non-kErrorNone status so `if (error != kErrorNone)` takes the true branch at ip6.cpp:981.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: W seeds all carry the constant 8-byte header prefix (offsets 0x00-0x07 collapse to one or two values), routing the message into `case kProtoUdp` or `case kProtoIcmp6` and exclusively reaching the ICMP6 handler at line 972-973 and the UDP error-drop branch at line 966, which produces error != kErrorNone (winner takes T=10 at line 981, loser takes T=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: L seeds have widely varied bytes at offsets 0x00-0x07 (e.g. byte 0 spans 80/81/7f, byte 6 spans 64/29, byte 7 spans 00/29) so the IPv6 header is malformed before HandlePayload; the loser only reaches `case kProtoUdp` with successful UDP processing (error stays kErrorNone) and never exercises the ICMP6 case (line 972-973 is W-only [W] in the per-role overlay), so error remains kErrorNone and line 981 never takes the true branch in 10/10 loser trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the multi-byte equality CMPs that parse the IPv6 header (next-header/length fields), reads the literal protocol constants from the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, and substitutes them into the input in one mutation step. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they cannot perform input-to-state substitution and must rely on havoc mutation to randomly hit a multi-byte exact prefix, which is exponentially unlikely within budget. The asymmetry is between I2S-equipped fuzzers (cmplog, vpc) and the rest, exactly matching the [delta: I2S] axis of both decisive pairs.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is `if (error != kErrorNone)` at ip6.cpp:981, true side globally blocked.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: Location ip6.cpp:981:9, Globally blocked side: T

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs share the I2S technique delta and resolve every trial vs zero loser trials.
Pair 2 also has I2S delta, supporting single_feature classification with one shared mechanism axis.
Only the winner reaches the ICMP6 dispatch case in HandlePayload (lines 972-973 are W-only in the per-role coverage overlay).
The error-true side of the blocker is reached only by W; L never takes the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 981  T=10 F=20  T=0 F=20  if (error != kErrorNone)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: W reaches the UDP error-drop branch (line 966) nine times while L never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 966  T=9 F=20  T=0 F=20  if (error == kErrorDrop)

evidence_trail.claim: All five winner-resolving seeds share the leading byte 0x78 at offset 0 (10/10) while L bytes at offset 0 are 80/81/7f, evidencing a multi-byte fixed-prefix gate.
Offset 0x06 is 0x10 in 10/10 W seeds but 0x64/0x29 in L seeds — a constant byte the winner must match.
Offset 0x07 is 0x11 (= kProtoUdp = 17) in 9/10 W seeds but 0x00 in 9/10 L seeds — the next-header protocol field.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace mutator reads CmpLogObserver and substitutes one operand of the CMP into the input, enabling exact multi-byte matches in one mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S; only edge-counter feedback and havoc mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; its CMP_MAP only buckets by Hamming/prefix distance.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness that gates a true branch on an 8-byte fixed IPv6-header-shaped equality CMP (where one operand is a runtime-loaded literal at the same address each call) and a downstream handler that returns a non-success error were built, and cmplog FAILED to resolve it while naive resolved it within the same budget, the I2SRandReplace attribution would be refuted.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP site whose operands I2S substitutes is not directly localized in the prompt — the chain from offset 0x06=0x10 to which OpenThread header field is parsed could be in the harness wrapper rather than in HandlePayload's call chain, so the 'multi-byte prefix' description is the right shape but the bytes may correspond to multiple smaller CMPs upstream.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace substitutes the 7-byte routing-locator IID anchor (00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc) from the CMP buffer into the interface-identifier field, satisfying the IsLocator conjuncts and the m8[6] range check in one mutation.
signature.one_line: I2S plants 7-byte locator IID anchor to satisfy IsRoutingLocator predicate

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain the 7-byte locator-IID magic prefix 00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc at offsets 0x21-0x27 so IsLocator() passes and m8[6] >= kAloc16Mask flips the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 7-byte magic constant sequence 00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc placed at the input offsets that map to the InterfaceIdentifier (here 0x21-0x27). The first four bytes satisfy m32[0] == HostSwap32(0x000000ff) and bytes 4-5 satisfy m16[2] == HostSwap16(0xfe00) (the IsLocator() equality CMPs), then byte 6 == 0xfc satisfies m8[6] >= kAloc16Mask (the blocked F side at line 256:28) while still clearing the reserved-bit mask at line 256:61.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five cmplog winner seeds carry the literal byte run 00 00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc at offset 0x20 (so the locator IID bytes land at 0x21-0x27). The BYTE DIFF section quantifies this: offset 0x24 is ff x10 in W, 0x25 is fe x10, 0x26 is 00 x10, 0x27 is fc x9 / ff x1 — i.e. every winner-resolving seed has the magic locator prefix and an m8[6] value >= 0xfc.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All five naive-blocking seeds have unrelated bytes at the same offsets: at 0x24 the L set is 00x3 c4x2 16x2 30x1 ..., at 0x25 it is 20x3 00x3 16x2 ..., at 0x26 it is 00x7 16x2 5cx1, at 0x27 it is 00x5 16x2 78x1 6dx1 — none of the 5 L seeds reconstruct the required ff fe 00 fc tail, so IsLocator() returns false and the chain short-circuits.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog instrument the integer CMPs inside IsLocator() (m32[0] == 0x000000ff and m16[2] == 0xfe00) and the m8[6] < 0xfc test at line 256:28. The post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer hands those constants to I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for the current operand bytes and substitutes the expected constant. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace (value_profile only widens edge feedback with CMP_MAP buckets, no operand substitution), so they must reconstruct seven specific bytes via blind havoc and almost never succeed within 12 h.

evidence_trail.claim: The branch under analysis is line 256 col 28 inside IsRoutingLocator(), whose source line combines IsLocator() with two byte tests on m8[6].
Both decisive pairs at this branch carry an I2S technique delta and both place cmplog-style fuzzers as winners against their I2S-less counterparts.
cmplog won the second decisive pair against naive with a one-technique I2S delta.
Every cmplog winner-resolving seed contains the literal byte sequence 00 00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc starting at input offset 0x20, anchoring the IsLocator magic prefix and the m8[6] = 0xfc byte.
The same 00 00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc prefix repeats in a second winner seed, confirming the pattern is shared across cmplog's resolving seeds and not a single-seed artifact.
Across all 10 winner seeds the byte at input offset 0x27 is fc nine times and ff once, i.e. always >= 0xfc (kAloc16Mask), so every W seed flips the false side of m8[6] < 0xfc.
Winner seeds carry the IsLocator m16[2] == 0xfe00 magic at offsets 0x24-0x25 unanimously (ff x10, fe x10), where the loser set is dispersed.
The m16[2] low byte 0x00 also appears at 0x26 in all winner seeds, completing the fe 00 fc trail required to keep IsLocator true and select the blocked side of the m8[6] < kAloc16Mask branch.
Loser-blocking seeds have unrelated bytes at the same offsets and never reconstruct the locator prefix.
The enclosing function is the locator-IID predicate and its only path to the blocked F side is the chained && expression.
cmplog's instrumentation captures the integer CMPs inside IsLocator and the m8[6] < 0xfc test, and its I2SRandReplace mutator can substitute those operands into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive carries no I2SRandReplace stage, so reconstructing the 7-byte magic must come from blind havoc/token mutations.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace as well — its CMP_MAP signal only widens feedback, it does not substitute operands.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates on the same 7-byte magic prefix 00 00 00 ff fe 00 fc but in which cmplog and value_profile_cmplog still cannot resolve the branch within the trial budget — that would show I2SRandReplace is not the deciding capability and some other property of the openthread harness is responsible.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The third sub-condition at col 61 ((m8[6] & kRloc16ReservedBitMask) == 0) is not directly evidenced as I2S-driven — I infer that fixing m8[6] = 0xfc happens to clear the reserved bits, but I do not have an explicit I2S substitution trace for that mask CMP.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace pastes the 7-byte ULA prefix run and header signature from CMP operands into the input, causing UpdateIp6Route to return an error code other than kErrorNone/kErrorAddressQuery so the switch default arm is reached.
signature.one_line: I2S plants ULA prefix bytes to drive switch past kErrorNone/kErrorAddressQuery

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must steer UpdateIp6Route to return an error code that is neither kErrorNone nor kErrorAddressQuery, requiring specific magic bytes in IPv6 header fields.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An IPv6 message payload whose header fields drive UpdateIp6Route() to return an error code that is neither kErrorNone nor kErrorAddressQuery (e.g. kErrorDrop / kErrorNoRoute / kErrorInvalidArgs). Concretely, the W seeds carry a fixed multi-byte literal at offsets 0x19-0x1f equal to `fd de ad 00 be ef 00` (a fd00::dead:beef-style IPv6 ULA prefix block) plus an early-offset header signature (`66 65 5c` = `fe\` at 0x01-0x03) — these bytes are constant across all 10 W seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog/vpc winners contain the exact 7-byte run `fd de ad 00 be ef 00` at offsets 0x19-0x1f in 10/10 seeds, and the `66 65` (`fe`) signature at offsets 0x01-0x02 in 10/10 seeds. With these constants in place, switch(error) at line 571 falls through case kErrorNone and case kErrorAddressQuery and reaches `default:` at line 582.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive/vp blocking seeds never produce the `fd de ad 00 be ef 00` run (loser bytes at 0x19-0x1f are dominated by `ff ff ff 00 00 00 00` or other random patterns) and lack the `fe\` header signature. Without these literal byte runs, UpdateIp6Route returns kErrorNone (the dominant case at line 573 in the loser cov dump: T=105 F=0) and ExitNow() fires before reaching the default arm.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace in cmplog/vpc reads operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer populated by trace_cmp* callbacks inside UpdateIp6Route() and Ip6 header parsing; the integer/memory comparisons against the ULA prefix constants (0xfd 0xde 0xad 0xbe 0xef) and the header magic are captured and substituted into the input via I2SRandReplace. naive/vp lack I2SRandReplace and therefore cannot synthesize these multi-byte literal constants within budget — chaining 4-7 byte runs together at fixed offsets requires direct operand substitution, which only cmplog/vpc provide through I2SRandReplace.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs at this branch are under the I2S technique delta.
The second decisive pair is also under I2S delta with cmplog winning over naive.
The blocker is the default arm of the switch(error) statement on UpdateIp6Route's return value.

Loser inputs overwhelmingly take the kErrorNone arm (T=105 F=0) while winner inputs take the default arm (T=19 F=60 on the kErrorNone case means most fall through to default; L=T=0 F=105 means loser never reaches default).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 573  T=60 F=19  T=105 F=0  case kErrorNone:

evidence_trail.claim: Loser never reaches the default arm at line 582 (W=T=19, L=T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 582  T=19 F=60  T=0 F=105  default:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: All 10 winner seeds contain the literal byte fd at offset 0x19, while only 1 of 10 loser seeds does.
All 10 winner seeds carry the literal byte de at offset 0x1a, while loser seeds dominate with 53 (`S`).
All 10 winner seeds carry the literal byte ad at offset 0x1b, while losers carry ff or 00.
All 10 winner seeds carry be ef 00 at offsets 0x1d-0x1f, never present together in losers.
All 10 winner seeds share the literal `f` (0x66) byte at offset 0x01 and `e` (0x65) at offset 0x02 (`fe` signature) — losers do not.
cmplog and value_profile_cmplog instrumentation captures CMP operands per execution and I2SRandReplace mutator substitutes those operands into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses Hamming-distance CMP_MAP gradient but lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot directly substitute multi-byte literals.
cmplog winning trial-1 has 10/10 resolution while naive has 9/10 blocking and value_profile 10/10 blocking, attributable to I2S delta.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness replaces UpdateIp6Route()'s error-returning code path with one whose distinguishing inputs are NOT compared as multi-byte literals (e.g. error returned when an accumulated checksum equals a runtime value), then cmplog should NOT retain its >=8/10 win over naive — if it still wins, the mechanism is not I2S substitution of constants but something else.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I am inferring from BYTE DIFF that the fd-de-ad-be-ef and `fe` patterns are operands of integer/memory CMPs inside UpdateIp6Route — I have not directly traced which CMP instruction the I2S find substituted into. The patterns are consistent with IPv6 ULA prefix and link-local SLAAC tokens but the exact CMP site is unconfirmed.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace captures the mesh-local prefix and 0xFFFE IID anchor from CMP operands and substitutes them across the IPv6 address bytes, satisfying IsMeshLocalAddress and IsRoutingLocator together to take the true return at line 3835.
signature.one_line: I2S substitutes mesh-local prefix and IID anchor to satisfy routing-locator test

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode an IPv6 address with mesh-local prefix AND the 0x000000FFFE00 routing-locator IID anchor at the IID middle bytes.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An IPv6 address whose lower 64 bits (IID) match the routing-locator anchor pattern 0x0000:00FF:FE00:XXXX (the modified EUI-64 with the fixed 'FF FE' middle marker and zero high-IID bytes) AND whose upper bits satisfy IsMeshLocalAddress. Concretely the winner seeds carry a 4-byte equality-gated run '00 00 00 00 ff fe 00' at offsets 0x1f-0x25 plus the 'be ef' / mesh-local prefix bytes at offsets 0x08-0x09 and 0x1d-0x1e.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five cmplog winner seeds have the identical concentrated multi-byte run '00 be ef 00 00 00 00 00 ff fe 00' across offsets 0x1c-0x26 (W shows that pattern with count 10/10 at offsets 0x19-0x26), which is the literal anchor compared inside the IsMeshLocalAddress and IsRoutingLocator predicate.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive/value_profile loser seeds at the same offsets 0x19-0x26 carry varied bytes dominated by 'ff' and ')' (0x29) padding with no occurrence of the 'ff fe 00' middle marker — without that 16-bit anchor and the mesh-local prefix, the && short-circuits at IsMeshLocalAddress(aAddress) returning false. The aggregate hitcount of the true branch is 134/167 for winners and 0 for losers.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CMP buffer recorded by integer-CMP interception when IsMeshLocalAddress and IsRoutingLocator perform their address-prefix and IID equality comparisons, then substitutes the literal mesh-local prefix and the 0xFFFE IID anchor into the input at the offsets where the loser carried random havoc bytes. Naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace; value_profile's CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient on a wide multi-byte address compare provides no clean gradient because the anchor is a fixed concatenation of constants spanning 8+ bytes, so even VP fails to drive the input to the exact pattern within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The branch is a boolean conjunction gating on both an address-prefix check and an IID routing-locator check.
Both decisive pairs are under the I2S technique axis with the augmented fuzzer winning 10/10 and the loser blocked.
cmplog resolves 10/10 trials and naive blocks in 9/10 trials at this branch.
Winner-resolving seeds share a multi-byte anchor 'be ef 00 ... 00 ff fe 00' spanning offsets 0x08 through 0x26.
The winner-side byte sets at offsets 0x19-0x1b are unanimous '\xfd\xde\xad' across all 10 sampled winner seeds, indicating a literal multi-byte equality constraint inside the predicate.
The winner side has unanimous 'be ef 00' bytes at offsets 0x1c-0x1e while losers vary.
Winner inputs are unanimous '\xff\xfe' at offsets 0x24-0x25, the fixed middle marker of the modified EUI-64 IID identifying a routing locator.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes one CMP operand with the other after scanning the input, which is exactly the operator needed to plant the FF:FE anchor at the correct offset.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive's mutator stack contains no I2S substitution mutator.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and relies only on CMP_MAP gradient.
The blocker's true branch hits zero in losers and 134 in value_profile_cmplog winners on average.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=134  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker line is hit by both W and L sets, but the true outcome differs — W takes the true path 42 times while L takes false 10 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3835  T=42 F=0  T=0 F=10  return IsMeshLocalAddress(aAddress) && aAddress.GetIid()....  <-- BLOCKER

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness implementing the same IsMeshLocalAddress && IsRoutingLocator gate at offsets 0x18-0x27 of the input where, at 12h budget × 10 trials, naive resolves the trap with the same probability as cmplog — that would show the multi-byte anchor is reachable by havoc alone and the I2S substitution is not the mechanism.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I claim the mesh-local prefix is the 'be ef' bytes at offsets 0x08 and 0x1d, but those bytes could be a coincidental marker in this test harness rather than the literal Thread mesh-local prefix constant; without reading mle.cpp's IsMeshLocalAddress implementation I cannot verify which exact constant is compared.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace pastes Thread protocol literals (mesh-local prefix fd de ad 00 be ef at 0x19-0x1e and IID anchor ff fe 00 fc at 0x24-0x27) from the CMP buffer in one mutation step, clearing the upstream parser chain to reach GetContext with a nonzero contextId.
signature.one_line: I2S plants dispersed Thread protocol literals to reach GetContext with nonzero id

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must encode a well-formed 6LoWPAN/Thread frame whose IPv6 address triggers a non-zero Context-ID lookup; deep parser-chain gates require I2S substitution of fixed protocol literals.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes that pass an upstream chain of equality CMPs against Thread-protocol fixed literals (notably the mesh-local IPv6 prefix bytes fd de ad 00 be ef at offsets 0x19-0x1e and IID anchor ff fe 00 fc at 0x24-0x27) so the parser reaches Lowpan::FindContextToCompressAddress and calls LeaderBase::GetContext with a non-zero aContextId, taking the false branch at line 177.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds share a stable byte signature at the same offsets: 0x19-0x1e = fd de ad 00 be ef (100% concentration across the 10 seeds shown in BYTE DIFF) and 0x24-0x27 = ff fe 00 fc (100%). cmplog's I2S substitutes these multi-byte protocol literals directly from the intercepted CMP buffer, so the parser reaches the GetContext call site and the contextId byte derived from the input is non-zero (offset 0x28 shows 01/40/43/0b in winners).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace; their havoc-only mutation stack cannot synthesize the multi-byte protocol literals (fd de ad 00 be ef, ff fe 00 fc) needed to clear the upstream parser chain. Loser seeds show dispersed random bytes at the same offsets (ff/87/29/random) and never reach the GetContext call with a non-zero contextId — all 10 trials block for 12.00h and never resolve.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog (and vpc) record each integer/memcmp callback's operands into a per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace scans the input for one operand and substitutes the other, pasting protocol literals like the Thread mesh-local prefix into the right offset in one mutation step. The lineage of a cmplog winner-resolving seed contains a depth-8 ParentInfo-only (mutation_op = -) entry, the current build's I2S-floor signal.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs share the I2S technique delta and the same winner/loser orientation (cmplog/vpc vs naive/vp).
The second decisive pair is also an I2S delta, justifying single_feature collapse.
The blocker is an equality comparison against a context-id constant; the false branch is the globally blocked side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:     if (aContextId == Mle::kMeshLocalPrefixContextId)
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The branch is reached via a deep call chain entering through Lowpan::FindContextToCompressAddress.
All 10 winner-resolving seeds share the byte fd at offset 0x19; all 10 share de at 0x1a, ad at 0x1b, 00 at 0x1c, be at 0x1d, ef at 0x1e — a fixed IPv6 prefix signature.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
0x0019  fd(.)x10                            ff(.)x10                            DIFFER
   0x001a  de(.)x10                            53(S)x4 ff(.)x3 c3(.)x2 16(.)x1     DIFFER
   0x001b  ad(.)x10                            ff(.)x8 16(.)x1 00(.)x1             DIFFER

evidence_trail.claim: Winners also share a four-byte ff fe 00 fc IID-anchor signature at offsets 0x24-0x27, characteristic of Thread/6LoWPAN address compression.
Winner inputs route through the while-loop after the blocker (line 183) instead of the true-branch mesh-local helper (lines 178-181); losers do the opposite.
The per-line overlay shows the while-loop continuation is W-only while the early return is L-only, confirming aContextId is zero for losers and non-zero for winners.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 177  T=0 F=25  T=47 F=0  if (aContextId == Mle::kMeshLocalPrefixContextId)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog and vpc carry I2SRandReplace; naive and value_profile do not.
value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot synthesize the multi-byte fixed literals even with its CMP_MAP gradient.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same multi-byte fd-de-ad-00-be-ef + ff-fe-00-fc literal gate ahead of a uint8_t equality test, where cmplog/vpc fail to resolve the deep branch within 12h while naive/vp succeed — would refute the I2S-substitution attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact byte-to-aContextId dataflow is inferred from the W/L hex-dump overlap with Thread mesh-local prefix and 6LoWPAN IID anchor patterns, not directly traced; aContextId could in principle be derived from a different offset than 0x28 in some seeds.
